message:
test(memdb): use values.first().cloned() in rowid-range test to avoid clippy index_panicking

Follow-up to 2d9a28b1's test
test_memtable_rowid_range_bounds_use_dense_offset_with_sparse_fallback.
The mapping closure used values[0].clone() to extract the first
column, which clippy flags as a potential panic site (index out of
bounds on an empty row). Switched to values.first().cloned() —
returns Option<SqliteValue> rather than panicking — and updated the
expected vec entries to wrap in Some(...). The semantic check is
unchanged (the test rows are seeded with one column, so .first() is
always Some) but clippy is now happy and the test is robust against
future changes that ever produce a column-less row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
